settings.text: Radiant barriers need an airspace to perform as intended.
Perforated radiant barrier is not a vapor barrier.
Thermal Wrap is for fully conditioned buildings where vapor, air, and radiant control matter together.
For cover and repair over old insulation, compare against BlueTex Pro or Supreme rolls.

settings.heading: Airspace
settings.text: Foil needs adjacent airspace.

settings.heading: Perforated
settings.text: Not a vapor barrier.

settings.heading: Thermal Wrap
settings.text: Vapor, air, and radiant control.

settings.heading: Quote Help
settings.text: Ask before choosing an unclear assembly.

settings.heading: Radiant or wrap assemblies
settings.text: Use when the assembly needs radiant control, vapor control, or Thermal Wrap performance.

settings.heading: Covering old insulation
settings.text: If the goal is a clean interior over existing insulation, compare BlueTex Pro or Supreme rolls.

settings.heading: Airspace and tape
settings.text: Radiant applications may need spacers and foil tape depending on the assembly.

settings.heading: Is This Right For Me?
settings.text: Barrier and wrap products solve different problems than BlueTex cover/repair rolls.

settings.heading: Foil + airspace
settings.text: Use when foil faces an open airspace for radiant heat control.

settings.heading: Not vapor barrier
settings.text: Perforated radiant barrier lets vapor pass.

settings.heading: Conditioned buildings
settings.text: For fully conditioned buildings needing vapor, air, and radiant control.

settings.heading: Cover and repair
settings.text: Choose BlueTex rolls if the goal is covering existing insulation with a clean finish.

settings.heading: Barrier Type Comparison
settings.text: Match the barrier type to the building assembly and project goal.

settings.heading: HDPE Furring Strip Spacers
settings.text: Creates airspace where the radiant barrier assembly calls for it.

settings.heading: Foil Seam Tape
settings.text: Pairs with foil-facing seams and radiant barrier applications.

settings.heading: BlueTex™ Pro or Supreme
settings.text: Better path when the customer needs moisture control or cover/repair instead of perforated radiant barrier.

settings.heading: Request a Material List
settings.text: Useful when the assembly or airspace requirement is unclear.

settings.heading: Complete the System
settings.text: Barrier and wrap products depend heavily on the assembly, airspace, and tape choice.

settings.heading: Application notes
settings.text: Radiant barrier performance depends on the reflective surface facing an airspace. Perforated radiant barrier is not a vapor barrier.

settings.heading: Specs
settings.text: Check the selected product and variant for facing, perforation, width, roll length, and coverage.

settings.heading: FAQ
settings.text: Choose Thermal Wrap for fully conditioned buildings. Choose Pro or Supreme rolls when the goal is cover and repair over existing insulation.

settings.heading: Details
settings.text: Assembly notes, specs, and barrier questions are available below.
